---
title: "System Tour"
---

## End-to-end data flow {#sec-system-tour}

The application is a pipeline with two asynchronous branches: the immediate
caption branch and the delayed correction branch.

```{mermaid}
sequenceDiagram
    participant AU as Core Audio AUHAL
    participant M as MicrophoneCaptureService
    participant V as VoxtralEmbeddedService
    participant C as ClassroomAppModel
    participant T as CaptionTimeline
    participant O as Overlay
    participant G as Gemma server
    participant B as Browser client

    AU->>M: native device frames
    M->>M: resample to PCM16 / 16 kHz / mono
    M->>V: ordered PCM chunk
    V->>C: provisional token accumulation
    C->>O: redraw latest text
    C->>B: SSE snapshot
    V->>C: finalized caption at boundary
    C->>T: append immutable raw segment
    C->>G: serialized correction request
    G-->>C: candidate + token usage
    C->>C: deterministic validation
    C->>T: attach correctedText
    C->>O: redraw corrected segment
    C->>B: SSE snapshot
```

### Process boundaries

The normal deployment contains two processes:

1. `ClassroomCaptions`, containing SwiftUI, AppKit, Core Audio, the C bridge,
   Voxtral weights, Metal resources, the HTTP server, and archives.
2. `mlx_lm.server`, containing Gemma and exposing only a loopback HTTP endpoint.

Voxtral is in-process because latency and stream control are critical. Gemma is
out-of-process because MLX-LM already supplies a maintained server and because
correction failure must be isolated from transcription.

### Swift Package targets

`Package.swift` defines:

- `ClassroomCaptionsCore`, a library without UI or audio frameworks;
- `CVoxtralEngine`, a C/Objective-C target linked to Accelerate and Metal;
- `ClassroomCaptions`, the executable depending on both;
- `VoxtralBenchmark`, an executable depending only on the C engine;
- `ClassroomCaptionsCoreTests`, which also imports the app target for integration
  tests.

This dependency direction is important:

```{mermaid}
flowchart BT
    Core[ClassroomCaptionsCore]
    CEngine[CVoxtralEngine]
    App[ClassroomCaptions]
    Bench[VoxtralBenchmark]
    Tests[Tests]
    App --> Core
    App --> CEngine
    Bench --> CEngine
    Tests --> Core
    Tests --> App
    Tests --> CEngine
```

`Core` cannot accidentally import AppKit or mutate a window. Its value types can
be tested without microphone permission, displays, Metal, or a network.

## State ownership

`ClassroomAppModel` is annotated `@MainActor` and `@Observable`. Conceptually it
is the single UI coordinator:

- owns the current `ClassroomSession` value;
- starts and stops services;
- translates service events into domain mutations;
- owns configurable UI state;
- publishes derived status to SwiftUI;
- ensures overlay and browser snapshots describe the same timeline.

Subsystems own their internal mutable state:

| Subsystem | Concurrency mechanism |
| --- | --- |
| UI coordinator | Swift main actor |
| Voxtral service | Dedicated serial `DispatchQueue` plus `Mutex` |
| WebSocket client | `Mutex` around connection state |
| microphone | Core Audio callback plus serial conversion queue |
| archive | Serial utility queue |
| local HTTP server | Serial Network.framework queue |
| Gemma correction | Swift `actor` and one application worker |

The application does not use a global “thread-safe” claim. Each boundary states
where mutation is legal.

## Session state machine

`ClassroomSession` is a value type with explicit phases:

```{mermaid}
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> starting
    starting --> listening: microphone ready
    listening --> paused: Pause
    paused --> listening: Resume
    listening --> stopping: Stop
    paused --> stopping: Stop
    failed --> stopping: cleanup
    stopping --> idle: model drained and archive written
```

Only `listening` accepts provisional text. Final captions are accepted while
`listening` and during `stopping`, because stopping the model may drain one last
caption. This small exception prevents truncating the final words.

## Configuration latching

Settings that affect an active model session are copied when the session starts:

- selected transcription backend;
- whether recording is enabled;
- microphone name;
- model delay.

This avoids a half-session where the UI picker says one backend while callbacks
belong to another. Display position and caption font can change live because
they affect rendering only.

## Failure philosophy

Failures are intentionally asymmetric:

- Voxtral or microphone failure stops useful captioning and is prominent.
- Gemma failure marks only that correction as failed; raw text remains.
- browser sharing failure does not stop local captions.
- archive failure is reported after the session but does not erase the
  in-memory timeline.

This ordering follows accessibility impact, not implementation convenience.
